slides.title: Introduction to RNNs
slides.content: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are a class of neural networks that specialize in processing sequences of data. They are called 'recurrent' because they perform the same operation for every element of a sequence, with the output depending on the previous computations.

slides.title: RNN Structure
slides.content: An RNN has a 'memory' that captures information about what has been calculated so far. This is achieved through loops within the network.

slides.title: Mathematics of RNNs
slides.content: The core formula of an RNN involves the state from the previous time-step and the current input.

slides.title: Applications of RNNs
slides.content:
RNNs are used in various applications such as:
- Language Modeling
- Speech Recognition
- Time Series Prediction
- Image Captioning

slides.title: Example: Text Generation
slides.content: Text generation using RNNs involves predicting the next word in a sequence given the previous words.

visual_assets.code:
import tensorflow as tf
text_model = tf.keras.Sequential([...])
# More code follows
visual_assets.caption: Basic RNN Text Generation Model

slides.title: Advantages vs. Limitations
slides.content: RNNs are powerful for sequence prediction but have limitations like gradient vanishing. Advanced architectures such as LSTM and GRU address these issues.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: RNNs have revolutionized the processing of sequential data despite their limitations. With advancements like LSTM and GRU, they continue to be a powerful tool in AI.
